symptom: Searches taking > 1-2 seconds

diagnostics.where to look: Redis SLOWLOG (SLOWLOG GET 128); optionally CONFIG GET slowlog-log-slower-than
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Slowlog entries dominated by FT.SEARCH/FT.AGGREGATE/vector queries with durations consistently around or above 1,000,000 usec (1s) for the same query pattern
diagnostics.fix for issue: Extract one representative slow query pattern to profile; temporarily tune slowlog-log-slower-than during debugging if needed

diagnostics.where to look: Index stats (FT.INFO index_name) attributes + metrics
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Schema/usage mismatch (querying or projecting fields not well supported), very large index sizes, high num_docs/num_records, GC stats suggesting pressure, vector algorithm/params not aligned with workload
diagnostics.fix for issue: Adjust schema to match query patterns (TAG/NUMERIC for exact match), avoid unnecessary indexed/sortable fields, and ensure vector field configuration matches intended latency/recall

diagnostics.where to look: Query profile (FT.PROFILE index_name SEARCH|AGGREGATE QUERY "..."), ideally with LIMIT 0 0 / NOCONTENT / LIMITED to reduce noise
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: High Total profile time and a clear top offender iterator/processor by Time/Counter/Size (e.g., broad TAG/TEXT/NUMERIC iterators, heavy UNION/INTERSECT, Loader/Sorter/Paginator dominating, VECTOR iterator dominating)
diagnostics.fix for issue: Apply the targeted fix for the dominating stage: tighten filters/selectivity, avoid leading wildcards, reduce projections (avoid LOAD */RETURN *), use cursor-based pagination instead of deep offsets, or tune vector search (index type/params/K)

diagnostics.where to look: Server resource stats (INFO, INFO CPU, INFO MEMORY); in clusters compare per-shard vs Coordinator times in FT.PROFILE
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: CPU saturation during the query, memory pressure, or (cluster) coordinator time dominated by one outlier shard
diagnostics.fix for issue: Scale vertically/horizontally and/or rebalance shards; separate heavy analytical workloads from latency-critical workloads when possible

diagnostics.where to look: (Advanced) Live capture (redis-cli MONITOR) for short windows
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Exact FT.SEARCH/FT.AGGREGATE commands and arguments that correlate with spikes; repeated expensive patterns
diagnostics.fix for issue: Use the captured command text as the input to FT.PROFILE; stop MONITOR immediately after capturing enough samples due to overhead

symptom: Search requests taking longer than expected (e.g. > 200-300 ms OLTP, > 1-2 s analytics)

diagnostics.where to look: Redis SLOWLOG (SLOWLOG GET) and/or application logs to pick a representative slow query
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Consistent slow entries for the same index/query shape versus only occasional spikes
diagnostics.fix for issue: Select a representative query string (same filters/sort/pagination) for profiling

diagnostics.where to look: FT.PROFILE output for that query
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Parsing/pipeline time is normally small; most time is in iterators and result processors; one stage dominates
diagnostics.fix for issue: Tune based on the dominating stage (tighten filters, reduce projection/loading, avoid deep pagination, avoid expensive wildcards/ORs, tune vector stage)

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Index design signals that match the profile (e.g., many fields loaded but not indexed, excessive SORTABLE, vector algorithm mismatch, large index sizes implying GC/memory pressure)
diagnostics.fix for issue: Update/rebuild schema and index design to match actual query and projection patterns

symptom: Client-side timeouts or HTTP 5xx/504s from services that depend on FT.SEARCH, FT.AGGREGATE, or vector KNN commands

diagnostics.where to look: Client/APM logs to capture the exact query pattern around timeouts
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Timeouts correlate to a specific endpoint/query shape (filters, SORTBY, pagination depth, KNN parameters)
diagnostics.fix for issue: Extract one exact failing query for server-side profiling

diagnostics.where to look: Redis SLOWLOG and FT.PROFILE for that query
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: SLOWLOG dominated by FT.* during incident; FT.PROFILE shows high Total profile time and/or (cluster) high coordinator time and per-shard outliers
diagnostics.fix for issue: Reduce server-side work per the dominating FT.PROFILE stage; in clusters, address hot/outlier shards (data skew, resource issues)

diagnostics.where to look: (Optional) Short MONITOR session
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Confirms the real commands sent by the app (including LIMIT/OFFSET, DIALECT, vector params) match expectations
diagnostics.fix for issue: Use captured commands for repeatable FT.PROFILE runs; keep MONITOR short-lived

symptom: High P95/P99 latency for search endpoints in APM dashboards, while non-search Redis commands (GET/SET/HGETALL) stay fast

diagnostics.where to look: SLOWLOG GET and APM traces
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Tail latency spikes align with FT.SEARCH/FT.AGGREGATE/vector commands while baseline Redis ops remain fast
diagnostics.fix for issue: Identify the top slow FT.* patterns to profile

diagnostics.where to look: FT.PROFILE of representative slow queries
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Broad iterators (large Size/Counter), heavy Loader cost (too much loading), Sorter/Paginator cost (deep pagination / large sorts), or vector stage dominating
diagnostics.fix for issue: Tighten filters/selectivity, reduce projections, switch deep offsets to FT.CURSOR where applicable, and tune vector/index settings

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Index size/cardinality and GC stats indicating pressure that can affect tail latency
diagnostics.fix for issue: Reduce unnecessary indexing/sortable fields, right-size resources, and plan index design around real queries

symptom: Redis CPU pegged (single core near 100%) when running certain queries, but not under normal load

diagnostics.where to look: INFO CPU and SLOWLOG
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: CPU spikes correlate with specific FT.* queries; slowlog durations increase during spikes
diagnostics.fix for issue: Pick the correlated query patterns for FT.PROFILE

diagnostics.where to look: FT.PROFILE iterator tree and result processors
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Expensive wildcard term expansion, heavy UNION/INTERSECT, unfiltered searches creating huge candidate sets, or vector iterator dominating
diagnostics.fix for issue: Constrain known anti-patterns (leading wildcards, too many ORs, unfiltered searches), add/selective structured filters, simplify boolean logic, and tune vector search parameters

diagnostics.where to look: Cluster view (if applicable): per-shard times vs coordinator time in FT.PROFILE
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: One shard is a clear outlier (hot shard / skew) or coordinator overhead becomes significant
diagnostics.fix for issue: Rebalance slots/add shards or address shard-specific resource issues

symptom: Redis SLOWLOG entries dominated by FT.SEARCH, FT.AGGREGATE, or vector-search commands

diagnostics.where to look: SLOWLOG GET and (optional) CONFIG GET/SET slowlog-log-slower-than
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Most entries are FT.* commands and the same shapes recur with high duration
diagnostics.fix for issue: Extract one representative query string and keep the slowlog threshold appropriate for debugging

diagnostics.where to look: FT.PROFILE for the top offenders
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: A single iterator/processor dominates (Loader/Sorter/Paginator/Vector/UNION, etc.)
diagnostics.fix for issue: Apply the fix corresponding to the dominant stage (schema/selectivity, wildcards/ORs, pagination, projection/loading, vector tuning, or resourcing)

symptom: Index won't create / FT.CREATE fails

diagnostics.where to look: FT.CREATE error reply and FT._LIST
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: FT.CREATE returns an error and the index is absent from FT._LIST
diagnostics.fix for issue: Fix the FT.CREATE statement (syntax/options/field definitions) for your deployed version, then re-run

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name (if it exists/partially exists)
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Index Errors / indexing failures indicate partial/failed creation or resource issues
diagnostics.fix for issue: Address reported errors (schema/data alignment, OOM) and recreate the index cleanly

symptom: FT.CREATE returning errors (syntax errors, unsupported options, invalid field definitions, version-related incompatibilities)

diagnostics.where to look: FT.CREATE error message
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Error text points to invalid syntax, unsupported flags, invalid field definitions, or incompatibilities
diagnostics.fix for issue: Update the FT.CREATE command to match the module/version and actual stored data types (HASH vs JSON, field types), then retry

symptom: FT.SEARCH / FT.AGGREGATE returning 0 results or obviously incomplete results right after index creation or bulk imports, even though keys exist with the right prefix

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: indexing: 1 and percent_indexed < 1 (background scan still running/stuck) and/or hash_indexing_failures > 0; Index Errors/field statistics identify failures
diagnostics.fix for issue: If indexing is progressing, wait for percent_indexed to reach 1; if failures are present, continue to schema/data diagnostics and fix before rebuilding

diagnostics.where to look: Index definition and keyspace alignment (FT.INFO index_definition + attributes; SCAN 0 MATCH "prefix:*" COUNT 1000)
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Prefix mismatch (user:* vs users:*), wrong key_type (ON HASH vs ON JSON), JSONPath identifiers not matching stored docs
diagnostics.fix for issue: Drop and recreate the index with correct PREFIX(es) and ON HASH/ON JSON; correct schema identifiers/paths

diagnostics.where to look: Failing document samples (HGETALL key / JSON.GET key $), especially FT.INFO 'last indexing error key'
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Type mismatches (NUMERIC stored as string), invalid GEO/WKT formats, vector DIM/TYPE mismatch, JSONPath resolving to arrays/objects
diagnostics.fix for issue: Fix invalid documents or adjust schema to match real data; rebuild the index when failures are widespread

diagnostics.where to look: Memory/OOM signals (INFO MEMORY; FT.INFO Index Errors background indexing status; logs)
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Index background scan did not complete due to OOM / background indexing status: OOM failure
diagnostics.fix for issue: Relieve memory pressure (increase memory where safe, reduce index scope/dataset, remove unused indexes) then recreate the index so background scan completes

symptom: In RESP3 replies, a warnings entry calls out partial indexes, indexing failures, or OOM-related issues for the queried index

diagnostics.where to look: RESP3 response warnings / FT.PROFILE Warnings
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Warnings explicitly mention partial data, indexing failures, background scan not complete, OOM failure, schema/type mismatches
diagnostics.fix for issue: Treat as an indexing health problem: move to FT.INFO to identify the root cause and correct it

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name Index Errors + field statistics
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: hash_indexing_failures rising; last indexing error key present; background indexing status indicates OOM or repeated failures
diagnostics.fix for issue: Inspect failing keys, fix schema/data alignment, address OOM if present, then rebuild/recreate the index to restore full coverage

symptom: In FT.PROFILE, the Warnings section reports partial data due to indexing failure, background scan did not complete, OOM failure, schema/type mismatches

diagnostics.where to look: FT.PROFILE Warnings
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Warnings explicitly call out partial index/OOM/schema mismatch
diagnostics.fix for issue: Use FT.INFO to locate Index Errors, failing fields, and last indexing error key

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name (Index Errors, field statistics, last indexing error key) + inspect failing key (HGETALL/JSON.GET)
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Failing key contains values incompatible with schema (NUMERIC/GEO/VECTOR/JSONPath mismatches)
diagnostics.fix for issue: Fix offending documents or adjust schema; then rebuild/recreate the index so percent_indexed reaches 1 and failures stop increasing

diagnostics.where to look: INFO MEMORY and FT.INFO background indexing status
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: OOM failure during background scan
diagnostics.fix for issue: Relieve memory pressure and recreate the index

symptom: FT.INFO index_name shows indexing: 1 for a long time (background indexing never completing)

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: indexing: 1 with percent_indexed not moving
diagnostics.fix for issue: Check Index Errors/hash_indexing_failures to decide whether to wait (healthy) or fix failures (unhealthy)

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO Index Errors + INFO MEMORY/CPU
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Background indexing status indicates OOM failure, or repeated indexing failures; resource pressure is high
diagnostics.fix for issue: Relieve resource pressure, fix data/schema mismatches, and recreate the index if the initial scan cannot complete

symptom: FT.INFO index_name shows percent_indexed < 1

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: percent_indexed < 1 along with indexing: 1 indicates initial background scan incomplete
diagnostics.fix for issue: If progressing, wait; if stalled, inspect Index Errors/hash_indexing_failures and address root causes (schema mismatch, OOM, prefix issues)

symptom: FT.INFO index_name shows hash_indexing_failures > 0

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name (hash_indexing_failures, Index Errors, field statistics, last indexing error key)
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Failures are non-zero and/or increasing; per-field errors point to a specific attribute; last indexing error key is present
diagnostics.fix for issue: Inspect failing keys (HGETALL/JSON.GET) and correct schema/data mismatches (types, GEO/WKT, vector DIM/TYPE, JSONPath shape)

diagnostics.where to look: After fixes, FT.INFO index health
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Failures stop increasing and percent_indexed reaches 1
diagnostics.fix for issue: If failures are widespread/historical, rebuild the index (FT.DROPINDEX + FT.CREATE) under stable conditions

symptom: FT.INFO index_name shows an Index Errors block and field statistics reporting indexing failures or a last indexing error and last indexing error key

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO Index Errors + field statistics
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Index Errors identify failing fields and provide a last indexing error key; background indexing status may indicate OOM
diagnostics.fix for issue: Inspect the reported key, fix the specific field/type/JSONPath/vector mismatch, and address OOM if indicated

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO after corrective action
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: hash_indexing_failures stabilizes and percent_indexed becomes 1
diagnostics.fix for issue: Recreate the index if it remains partial or cannot recover

symptom: FT.DROPINDEX <index> DD not removing all documents with the expected prefix because some documents were never successfully indexed

diagnostics.where to look: Before relying on DD: FT.INFO index_name
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: percent_indexed < 1 and/or hash_indexing_failures > 0 indicates the index does not fully cover the keyspace
diagnostics.fix for issue: Fix indexing health (schema/data/prefix/OOM) and rebuild until coverage is complete

diagnostics.where to look: After the index is healthy: FT.DROPINDEX <index> DD
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: DD now deletes the expected set of documents because they were actually indexed
diagnostics.fix for issue: Use DD only when indexing is complete and failures are addressed

symptom: Redis OOM / out-of-memory

diagnostics.where to look: INFO MEMORY
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Memory usage is near configured limits / OOM behavior observed
diagnostics.fix for issue: Relieve memory pressure (increase memory/maxmemory where safe, reduce dataset/index scope, remove unused keys/indexes)

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO Index Errors/background indexing status (for indexes that are building)
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Index background scan did not complete due to OOM / background indexing status: OOM failure
diagnostics.fix for issue: Recreate affected indexes once memory is stable so background indexing completes

symptom: Documents missing or results look outdated

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name (num_docs, indexing, percent_indexed, hash_indexing_failures) and RESP3/FT.PROFILE Warnings
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: percent_indexed < 1 / indexing: 1 (background scan not finished) and/or warnings about partial data due to indexing failure/OOM
diagnostics.fix for issue: Wait for indexing to complete if healthy; if partial/failing, move to schema/data/prefix and OOM diagnostics and rebuild as needed

diagnostics.where to look: Keyspace alignment (FT.INFO index_definition prefixes/key_type) and SCAN MATCH prefix:*
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Prefix mismatch or wrong ON HASH/ON JSON results in many existing keys never being indexed
diagnostics.fix for issue: Drop and recreate index with correct PREFIX(es) and key_type

diagnostics.where to look: Index Errors/field statistics and failing keys (HGETALL/JSON.GET)
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Schema/data mismatches causing documents to be rejected during indexing
diagnostics.fix for issue: Fix offending documents or adjust schema; rebuild/reindex so missing docs become searchable

symptom: Vector KNN is slow or irrelevant

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO index_name attributes for the vector field
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Vector algorithm (FLAT vs HNSW) and params do not match latency/recall goals; DIM/TYPE mismatch risks indexing failures
diagnostics.fix for issue: Align vector algorithm/params with requirements; ensure DIM/TYPE match query vectors

diagnostics.where to look: FT.PROFILE of the vector query
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: VECTOR iterator / Vector Filter processors dominate Time/Counter
diagnostics.fix for issue: Reduce K, add complementary selective filters to cut candidates, and move from brute-force (FLAT) to approximate (HNSW) where appropriate

diagnostics.where to look: FT.INFO hash_indexing_failures + failing keys
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: Indexing failures indicate invalid vector contents/dimensions/types or JSONPath shape mismatch
diagnostics.fix for issue: Fix vector producers/data and rebuild the index if many vectors failed to index

symptom: Module errors, auth failures, connection err

diagnostics.where to look: Not covered in this guide yet (Quick Reference marks this section as 'to be added')
diagnostics.what pattern to look for to identify sou: N/A
diagnostics.fix for issue: N/A
